question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @VisibleForTesting clear() method on AbstractStreamFileParser sets last to null, and if this method is somehow reachable (e.g., via a test endpoint left enabled in production), an attacker can reset the in-memory hash anchor, causing the next parse() call to fall back to streamFileRepository.findLatest() which may return a stale or manipulated DB record as the chain anchor?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Downloader's lastStreamFile is an AtomicReference initialized to Optional.empty(), so that on the very first download after a restart, verifyHashChain() receives a null expectedPreviousHash, SHA_384.isHashEmpty(null) returns true, and any stream file with any previousHash passes verification and reaches onEnd(), allowing history injection at startup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file whose getName() contains path traversal characters (e.g., '../') so that when Utility.archiveFile() is called with the filename before onEnd(), the file is written to an unintended location, and the subsequent onEnd() call anchors this maliciously-named file as a valid chain position in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that in shouldParse(), the hash chain check uses previous.getType().isChained() (the PREVIOUS file's type), not the current file's type, so that if an attacker can cause a non-chained file to be the previous anchor, the current file's hash chain is never verified and onEnd() is called with an unverified tampered file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Retryable annotation on RecordFileParser.parse() will retry on failure, and if a tampered stream file causes a transient exception in onEnd() on the first attempt but succeeds on retry (e.g., due to a race condition in the DB), the tampered file is persisted as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a getLoadStart() or getLoadEnd() returning null, causing a NullPointerException in the totalDurationMetric.record() call after onEnd() in AbstractStreamFileParser.parse(), which propagates as an uncaught exception that prevents last.set() from being called, leaving the hash chain anchor in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain() skips all verification when cutoverService.getLastRecordFile() returns empty (last == null), so that during the initial block stream ingestion with no prior record file, any block file with any previousHash passes and reaches onEnd(), allowing arbitrary Hashgraph history to be injected as the genesis block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that in BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain(), when the last file is a record file and the new block has no wrapped record file (the 'First block after cutover' branch), blockFile.setPreviousHash(previousHash) overwrites the block's previousHash with the record file's hash without verifying the original previousHash, allowing a tampered block to silently adopt a valid hash chain position?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream file with a getItems() list containing duplicate consensus timestamps, causing SqlEntityListener.onEnd()'s flush() to attempt to insert duplicate records into the database, and if the DB constraint violation is caught and swallowed as a ParserException, the partial flush leaves the Hashgraph history in an inconsistent state while the chain anchor is still updated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFile.getHash() default implementation returns null, and if a concrete StreamFile subclass fails to override it, the hash stored after onEnd() via last.set() is null, causing SHA_384.isHashEmpty(null) to return true for all subsequent files, permanently disabling hash chain verification for the rest of the session?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a getConsensusStart() equal to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the Instant.ofEpochSecond(0L, streamFile.getConsensusEnd()) call after onEnd() to throw a DateTimeException, which propagates as an uncaught exception that prevents the hash chain anchor from being updated and causes the importer to stall?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SyntheticCryptoTransferApprovalMigration.onEnd() catches all exceptions and logs them without rethrowing, so that if the migration is triggered by a crafted stream file and fails silently, the migration's AtomicBoolean executed is set to true and the migration never runs again, permanently corrupting the historical crypto transfer approval data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CompositeRecordStreamFileListener iterates listeners by index (for loop with get(i)) rather than using an iterator, so that if a listener modifies the underlying list during onEnd() (e.g., via Spring context manipulation), the iteration skips or double-processes listeners, causing inconsistent Hashgraph history persistence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a getPreviousHash() that is exactly 96 hex characters of all zeros (the emptyHash value for SHA_384), causing SHA_384.isHashEmpty() to return true in verifyHashChain(), bypassing the hash chain check and allowing the file to be accepted and persisted via onEnd() regardless of the actual previous file's hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that in the batch parse(List<T>) path, if all files in the batch are skipped by shouldParse() (e.g., all have consensusEnd >= next consensusStart), doFlush() is never called and onEnd() is never invoked, but the in-memory last reference is not updated, allowing a subsequent batch with tampered files to use the stale anchor and pass hash chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileParser.parse() calls parserContext.clear() in a finally block even when onEnd() throws an ImporterException, so that the context is cleared before the exception propagates, preventing any retry mechanism from re-processing the tampered file's items and leaving the DB in a partially written state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a getBytes() returning a very large byte array (e.g., Integer.MAX_VALUE bytes) to cause an OutOfMemoryError during onEnd() processing, which is excluded from the @Retryable annotation's excludes list and thus propagates uncaught, crashing the importer and creating a window where the hash chain anchor is in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFile.copy() method used in Downloader.onVerified() creates a shallow copy, so that if the original streamFile's hash fields are mutable and modified after copy() but before onEnd() is called, the hash stored in lastStreamFile differs from the hash used for chain verification, creating a desynchronization that allows tampered files to pass?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain() only checks previousWrappedRecordBlockHash when last.getWrappedRecordBlockHash() is non-null, so that if an attacker can cause the previous record file to be stored with a null wrappedRecordBlockHash, the wrapped record block hash chain check is skipped and a tampered block with any previousWrappedRecordBlockHash passes verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a getName() that matches an already-processed file's name but with a different hash, exploiting the consensusEnd >= consensusStart skip logic in shouldParse() to silently discard the tampered file, but if the skip logic has an off-by-one error (>=  vs >), the tampered file could be processed and onEnd() called, overwriting the legitimate chain anchor?
